steps.title: Read the three segments of the graph
steps.content:
Segment 1 (hours 0–1): distance rises from 0 to a fixed value along a straight line — constant speed moving away from home.
Segment 2 (hours 1–5): distance stays flat — Quinidra is stationary for 4 hours.
Segment 3 (hours 5–6): distance falls in a straight line back to 0 — constant speed returning home.

steps.title: Eliminate choices based on segment shapes
steps.content:
Straight lines mean constant speed; curves mean changing speed. All three segments in the graph are straight lines, so any choice describing "increasing speed" or "decreasing speed" (curves) is wrong.
  • C: says increasing speed then decreasing speed — curved segments. Eliminated.
  • D: says increasing speed and decreasing speed — curved segments. Eliminated.

steps.title: Distinguish A from B on the third segment
steps.content:
Both A and B describe segment 1 (constant speed away) and segment 2 (4 hours at location) identically.
  • A: claims she "continued to drive away from her home" in segment 3 — but the graph shows distance returning to 0, meaning she drove back home. Eliminated.
  • B: claims she "drove back to her home at a constant speed" — distance decreases at a constant rate back to 0. That matches choice B — B is correct.
